risk_domain.name: Token Approval Phishing
risk_domain.description: Phishing attacks exploiting ERC-20/ERC-721 token approval mechanisms to drain user assets

limitations: cannot forge signatures without private key
malicious contracts are eventually flagged by security databases

H1_unlimited_approval.name: Unlimited token approval
H1_unlimited_approval.description: Transaction approves a spender for MAX_UINT256 (unlimited) tokens. The spender can drain the entire token balance at any time, indefinitely.

signals.name: max_uint_approval
signals.description: approve() or increaseAllowance() called with amount >= 2^255

signals.name: permit_unlimited
signals.description: EIP-2612 permit() or Permit2 signed with unlimited amount

recommendations.description: Set approval to exact amount needed for this transaction, not unlimited
recommendations.user_cost: may need re-approval for future transactions

recommendations.description: If using Permit2, set a short expiration (1 hour) on the approval

H1_unlimited_approval.fundamental_limitation: Some protocols require unlimited approvals to function (e.g., DEX routers). Limiting breaks these flows. The real fix is protocol-level: ERC-20 should support transaction-scoped approvals.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Dataset of N approval transactions, mix of limited and unlimited
benchmark_scenario.baseline: ~60% of DeFi approvals are unlimited (Etherscan data)

H2_unverified_spender.name: Unverified spender contract
H2_unverified_spender.description: Approval targets a contract that is not verified on Etherscan, not in any known protocol registry, or was deployed very recently.

signals.name: unverified_source
signals.description: Spender contract source code not verified on block explorer

signals.name: recently_deployed
signals.description: Spender contract deployed within last 24 hours

signals.name: no_protocol_registry
signals.description: Spender not found in any known protocol registry (DeFi Llama, Etherscan labels)

recommendations.description: Do not approve tokens to unverified contracts. Wait for source verification.
Simulate the transaction to see what the contract does before approving

H2_unverified_spender.fundamental_limitation: New legitimate contracts are always unverified initially. Time-based heuristics create a bootstrapping problem for new protocols.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Mix of verified (legitimate) and unverified (mix of legitimate new + malicious) contracts
benchmark_scenario.metric: True positive rate for malicious contracts, false positive rate for legitimate new contracts
benchmark_scenario.baseline: ~15% of phishing targets are unverified contracts

H3_known_scam_address.name: Known scam address
H3_known_scam_address.description: Spender or recipient address appears in a public scam/phishing database (Forta, Scam Sniffer, ChainAbuse).

signals.name: scam_database_hit
signals.description: Address found in scam/phishing database with confirmed reports

signals.name: similar_to_known_scam
signals.description: Address bytecode or deployment pattern matches known scam template

recommendations.description: Block the transaction. This address is confirmed malicious.

H3_known_scam_address.fundamental_limitation: Scam databases are reactive -- new scam addresses are not in the database until reported. Zero-day phishing contracts evade this check entirely.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Set of known scam addresses + legitimate addresses
benchmark_scenario.metric: 100% detection for known scams, 0% false positives
benchmark_scenario.baseline: databases cover ~70% of reported phishing addresses

H4_suspicious_function.name: Suspicious function selector
H4_suspicious_function.description: Transaction calls a function with an unusual or obfuscated selector not matching any known standard interface.

signals.name: unknown_selector
signals.description: Function selector not found in 4byte.directory or common interface databases

signals.name: multicall_with_approval
signals.description: Multicall/batch that includes hidden approval among other operations

detection.threshold: multicall_with_approval triggers BLOCK, unknown_selector triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Decode the calldata and show the user exactly what operations will execute
Block multicalls that contain approval operations not explicitly requested by the user

H4_suspicious_function.fundamental_limitation: Sophisticated phishing contracts can use proxy patterns or delegatecall to hide the actual approval logic from static analysis.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Mix of normal DeFi function calls and known phishing calldata patterns
benchmark_scenario.metric: Detection rate for hidden approvals in multicalls

H5_stale_approval.name: Stale token approval
H5_stale_approval.description: User has outstanding unlimited approvals to contracts they haven't interacted with in >30 days. These are dormant attack surface.

signals.name: stale_unlimited
signals.description: Unlimited approval to a contract with no user interaction in >30 days

signals.name: high_value_exposed
signals.description: Stale approval exposes >$1000 in token value

recommendations.description: Revoke approvals to contracts you haven't used in 30+ days
recommendations.user_cost: gas for revocation tx (~46K gas per revoke)

recommendations.description: Run weekly audit of all outstanding approvals and revoke unnecessary ones

H5_stale_approval.fundamental_limitation: Revocation costs gas. Users with many stale approvals face significant cost to clean up. Protocol-level expiring approvals would solve this.

benchmark_scenario.setup: User with N historical approvals, varying staleness and value exposed
benchmark_scenario.metric: Total value at risk from stale approvals before/after remediation
benchmark_scenario.baseline: average DeFi user has 10-50 outstanding approvals

approval_limiter.description: Replace unlimited approval amount with exact amount needed for current operation

tx_simulator.description: Simulate transaction execution and show all state changes including approvals

approval_revoker.description: Generate and submit revocation transactions for stale approvals

approval_auditor.description: Scan all outstanding approvals, calculate value at risk, flag stale ones

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring phishing detection rate

methodology.step_1: Collect dataset of known phishing transactions (Forta alerts, Scam Sniffer reports)
methodology.step_2: Collect dataset of legitimate DeFi approval transactions
methodology.step_4: Measure: true positive rate (phishing caught), false positive rate (legitimate flagged)

combined_benchmark.target: Catch >90% of known phishing patterns with <5% false positive rate

templates.summary: Scanned {n_txs} approval transactions. {n_risky} flagged ({pct_risky}%). Value at risk: ${value_at_risk}. Top risks: {top_risks}.
